threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still flinch when you hear the 38 bus driver shout, 'How many times do I have to say it?' It's a rite of passage to just roll with it.
Caught a glimpse of a couple arguing over a spilled drink on the 68 last Saturday. She was fuming, but he just kept laughing. Classic Brixton chaos.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I stepped out to the smell of fresh falafel and heard a street musician playing a quirky cover of 'Wonderwall' on an accordion.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Kreuzberg, you can spot a Berliner casually sipping coffee while waiting for a keelboat race on the canal – total vibe only locals get.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they can name three taco trucks on Mission Street and know their schedules by heart, even the ones who disappear at midnight.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a tux trying to calm down a dog that bolted on the Civic Center tracks. Only in SF do we get fancy and furry drama in one breath.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: They think 'tapas' means a plate of Olives everywhere. Most of us prefer a good bocadillo over the tourist traps, any day.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones standing confused at the wrong bus stop, still clutching their map like it’s a lifeline. Classic rookie move.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think buying a hot drink means stepping into a café, not queuing at a stall with thirty people, right?
Last night on the 59, a guy was singing to a half-empty bus like he was headlining Glastonbury. You could tell he was on his way back from a wild night.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The first time I saw a toddler in a pink tutu chasing a pigeon while their dad brewed coffee at the park. That's when it hit me.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You know that feeling when you’re on the U-Bahn and suddenly it stops for no reason? Just another day in Kreuzberg, where the trains seem to think it's on vacation.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local when they know to avoid the Civic Center BART during rush hour due to random outages. Tourists just look confused.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a tuxedo playing a saxophone on the bus while the driver was stuck in traffic. Only in SF can you get a live concert with your commute.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just quirky bars and plazas, but they miss the quiet morning rituals at the local bakeries where we share the day's gossip over coffee.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by how they still take photos of the vermut at 12:30 on a Sunday. Locals know it's all about the company, not the Instagram shot.
